title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, Failure Ambiguity, and Republish · Compiler Case: Confirm Queue Semantics
summary: A producer opens a normal channel, calls waitForConfirms after every message, discards sequence identity, and blindly republishes all work after a connection loss even when the broker may already own it. Planned artifact: Add and verify the confirm queue semantics increment in confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain when confirms occur for unroutable messages, persistent classic queues, quorum queues, and multiple routed queues.
Bound unconfirmed count, bytes, and age while preserving throughput, memory, fairness, and per-message outcome evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Confirm Queue Semantics · Theory Confirm Queue Semantics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6015 in a accessible municipal task intake with two worker pools. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
